\section{Timeline (auto-generated from git)}
\input{generated/timeline}

\section{Narrative notes}

\paragraph{2026-06 --- Paraglider data acquisition complete.}
The full FFVL paraglider CFD archive (\texttt{parapente.ffvl.fr}, seasons 1999--2000 to
2025--2026) has been scraped: about 203{,}000 flight records, of which $\sim$186{,}000
carry a GPS track. All 186{,}052 available tracks were downloaded.
\emph{What worked:} impersonating a Chrome TLS fingerprint (\texttt{curl\_cffi}) reliably
passes the FFVL Cloudflare challenge; the resumable, atomic, validated download survived
many interruptions without corruption.
\emph{What did not / gotchas:} 173 tracks could not be obtained --- 172 because the server
returns something that is not a valid IGC (broken or placeholder links on FFVL's side, so
a retry does not help), and 1 due to a transient Cloudflare challenge (worth a later
retry). On macOS/exFAT, the OS scatters \texttt{.\_*} sidecar files next to every written
file; a \texttt{clean} step removes them.

\paragraph{2026-06 --- Integrity verified.}
A full on-disk re-scan (\texttt{verify}) confirmed that all 186{,}052 downloaded
files are structurally valid IGC: no truncated files, no leftover \texttt{.part}, no read
errors. The verification logic was promoted from a throwaway script to a first-class
\texttt{soaring-para verify} subcommand (reusing the download-time validator), with tests.

\paragraph{2026-06 --- Robustness of the data path.}
The external disk remounted under a different name (\texttt{HDD\_DISANTE} $\to$
\texttt{SSD\_DISANTE}), which would have silently broken a hard-coded path. Fix: removed
every machine-specific path from the repo (placeholder + \texttt{SOARING\_PARA\_DATA\_ROOT}
environment variable) and added a start-up guard that aborts with a clear, actionable
message when \texttt{data\_root} is unset/unmounted --- and verified that \texttt{/Volumes}
is not user-writable, so a wrong path can never cause a silent download to the wrong place.

\paragraph{2026-07 --- Hang-glider data acquired; naming corrected.}
The hang-glider CFD (\texttt{delta.ffvl.fr}, seasons 2001--2002 to 2025--2026) was added
as a second source. The acquisition code (\texttt{soaring.acquisition.ffvl}) is shared; a
second CLI entry point \texttt{soaring-delta} (config \texttt{delta\_download.yaml}, env
var \texttt{SOARING\_DELTA\_DATA\_ROOT}) drives it. Data: $\sim$9{,}300 flights,
6{,}716 tracks downloaded, 30 failures (broken server-side links, same pattern as
paragliders). Stored on the SSD at \texttt{/Volumes/SSD\_DISANTE/hang\_gliders/delta\_cfd\_igc/}.
At the same time the paraglider CLI was renamed from the generic \texttt{soaring-ffvl} to
\texttt{soaring-para} (and \texttt{SOARING\_FFVL\_DATA\_ROOT} $\to$
\texttt{SOARING\_PARA\_DATA\_ROOT}), and the config was renamed from
\texttt{ffvl\_download.yaml} to \texttt{para\_download.yaml}, to avoid ambiguity now that
both sources are present.

\paragraph{2026-07 --- Hang-glider catalog built; datasets reorganized symmetrically.}
Ran the full pipeline on the hang-glider data (\texttt{soaring-delta clean}, \texttt{verify},
\texttt{build-catalog}, \texttt{status}), which so far had only been downloaded: 9{,}259
flights, 6{,}746 with a track, 6{,}716 downloaded and all verified (0 integrity failures), 30
unrecoverable server-side links. The local \texttt{data/} folder was reorganized to mirror the
SSD -- one directory per discipline (\texttt{paragliders/}, \texttt{hang\_gliders/}), each with
a versioned \texttt{seasons\_index.csv} and a git-ignored \texttt{catalog.csv}.
\texttt{generate\_stats.py} now reads both indices and emits per-discipline macros
(\texttt{\textbackslash StatPara*}, \texttt{\textbackslash StatHang*}) and two per-season
tables, and the first three thesis chapters were made symmetric across the two disciplines.

\paragraph{2026-07 --- Altitude channel decision: barometric.}
Decided to take the vertical dynamics from the \emph{barometric} altitude rather than the GNSS
altitude, diverging from the reference pipeline (J\'er\'emie's code derives $z$ and $v_z$ from
\texttt{AltGNSS}, reading but not using \texttt{AltPre}). Rationale: the pressure altitude has
sub-metre relative precision and low high-frequency noise, its errors being slow (offset,
drift) and killed by differencing, whereas the GNSS vertical carries a high-frequency noise
floor that derivatives amplify. Confirmed empirically with a new noise diagnostic -- an IGC
\texttt{B}-record parser (\texttt{soaring.analysis.igc}) and an altitude-noise analysis
(\texttt{soaring.analysis.altitude\_noise}) whose Welch power spectra show the GNSS floor two
to three orders of magnitude above the barometric one near Nyquist (with a thesis appendix on
the method). \emph{Consequences:} the \texttt{A}/\texttt{V}
validity flag is not a separate rule but is subsumed by the missing-altitude check on the
adopted channel (a \texttt{V} fix keeps position + baro on a baro flight, but is dropped on a
GNSS-fallback flight); one altitude channel per trajectory, no baro/GNSS splicing; flights
with no pressure sensor fall back to unfiltered GNSS, tagged by a per-flight
\texttt{alt\_source} column (fractions --- a substantial minority, larger for paragliders ---
to confirm on a larger sample once the SSD is remounted); the trimming threshold is on the
horizontal speed only ($v_{xy}>5$\,m/s, sustained); and the cleaning/trimming run on the raw
geographic coordinates (great-circle speeds), with the ENU conversion done \emph{last},
origin at take-off. Also redesigned the noise figure (dropped the redundant HF-index panel;
panel (a) now shows the GNSS$-$baro difference).

\paragraph{2026-07 --- Baro-absence fraction: sized sampling, not a full sweep.}
A full-population parse of the paraglider archive (186{,}052 files) for the
baro-channel-absence figure took an unacceptable $\sim$45\,min serially; a first fix
(parallelising across 8 processes) cut this to $\sim$15\,min, still too slow to be run
routinely. Replaced it with a properly justified \emph{simple random sample}: the standard
proportion sample-size formula ($n=z^2p(1-p)/e^2$, conservative $p=0.5$) gives $n=2401$ for
a target 95\%-confidence margin of error of $\pm2$ percentage points --
\texttt{required\_sample\_size} in \texttt{soaring.analysis.altitude\_noise}, with
\texttt{proportion\_ci} reporting the resulting point estimate and interval. The population
is treated as effectively infinite (no finite-population correction): at $n=2401$ out of
$186{,}052$ paraglider flights ($\sim$1.3\% sampled), the correction would move $n$ by only
about 30, not worth the extra parameter. The hang-glider archive (6716 files) stays a full
census: cheap enough (under two minutes) that there is nothing to gain from sampling it.
Total runtime: $\sim$50\,s. Final figures: paragliders $(28.4\pm1.8)\,\%$ (95\% CI,
sampled), hang gliders $17.5\,\%$ (exact, census) -- consistent with the earlier, less
rigorously sized
estimates. \emph{Also caught mid-flight:} the IGC parser accepted syntactically
well-formed but semantically invalid \texttt{B} records (e.g.\ minute $\geq 60$, hour
$\geq 24$, out-of-range latitude/longitude) without rejecting them; added explicit
format-validity checks (9 new tests) and corrected the thesis wording that had
conflated this \emph{implemented} format-validity layer with the fix-level
\emph{dynamics-plausibility} layer of Table~4.1, which is designed (thresholds exist as
a dataclass) but not yet implemented as code -- an important distinction the thesis now
states explicitly rather than glossing over.

\paragraph{2026-07 --- Pre-processing made data-driven and config-driven.}
Reworked Chapter~4 so the flight-level filtering is computed from the \emph{full parsed
dataset} (paragliders and hang gliders), not the declared catalog metadata: a track scan
(\texttt{soaring.analysis.preprocessing.scan\_tracks}) parses every downloaded track, and
Figure~4.2 shows the real duration and flown-path-length distributions with per-variable
\emph{marginal} retention curves. The cuts are duration $\geq 40$ min and path length
$\geq 30$ km (a minimal cut, $\sim$95\% retained); the earlier min-fix-count cut was dropped as
redundant with the duration cut, and the \emph{extent} proxy was replaced by the total flown
\emph{path length} (median 86 km para, 158 km hang). \emph{Key decision:} every pre-processing
threshold now lives in
\texttt{configs/preprocessing.yaml} (never hard-coded), loaded via
\texttt{load\_preproc\_config} into typed dataclasses. Also added: the great-circle (haversine)
formulas giving inter-fix speed and distance \emph{before} the ENU conversion; a
Savitzky--Golay smoothing/differentiation section with a noise-matched procedure for its two
hyperparameters; and the switch from a common resampling cadence to native per-flight
$\Delta t$ (Figure~4.5). An implementation blueprint
(\texttt{docs/guide/preprocessing-pipeline.md}) records the exact schema and steps, to retire
into code once the pipeline is built.

\paragraph{2026-07 --- Gap diagnostics; scan cache; baro-presence consolidated.}
Added Figure~4.5, the sampling-regularity diagnostics (largest gap relative to native
$\Delta t$, missing fraction of a uniform grid, each with its marginal retention curve),
fulfilling a promise Sec.~4.1.6 already made (``its retained fraction reported with the
others'') but had not yet delivered. Since a fresh full parse for this would have repeated
the $\sim$15--20\,min paraglider census just done for Figure~4.2, the per-flight scan is now
\textbf{cached} to \texttt{data/<discipline>/track\_scan.parquet} (gitignored, like
\texttt{catalog.csv}; \texttt{load\_or\_scan\_tracks} reads it if present, else scans and
writes it) -- later moved to the SSD, see below. While at it, \texttt{track\_stats} was
extended with a few near-free byproducts
of the same scan -- barometric-presence fraction, max horizontal/vertical speed, barometric
altitude range -- available for future validation of Table~4.1's fix-level bounds against
real data.

\emph{Consolidation:} the barometric-presence fraction (Sec.~4.1.1) now reads from this same
cache (\texttt{altitude\_noise.baro\_presence\_from\_scan}) instead of running its own
separate scan, turning the paraglider number from a sized sample into an exact census at no
extra cost: $30.1\,\%$ (exact, $n=186{,}025$) -- consistent with the earlier
sampled estimate, $(28.4\pm1.8)\,\%$, which correctly bracketed it. \emph{Also caught}: a
pre-existing fragility in \texttt{altitude\_noise.collect} where a single unreadable file
(a transient external-disk hiccup surfaced by two heavy scans back to back) aborted the
whole PSD sample; it now skips and logs the file instead.

\paragraph{2026-07 --- SSD reorganised; no data kept locally; figure fixes.}
\emph{Policy going forward: raw data, catalogs and every analysis byproduct live only on
the external SSD, never in the local repo.} Reorganised both discipline's \texttt{data\_root}
by maturity -- \texttt{raw/\{igc,raw\_xml\}} (untouched acquisition output),
\texttt{catalog/\{catalog.csv,seasons\_index.csv\}} (tables derived from it),
\texttt{derived/} (further byproducts, today just \texttt{track\_scan.parquet}; future
cleaned/filtered data and analysis results will get their own \texttt{processed/} and
\texttt{analysis/} siblings) -- and updated \texttt{Config} accordingly (new
\texttt{derived\_dir} property). Verified file counts before/after the move on both SSDs
(paraglider: 186{,}052 tracks, 27 XMLs; hang glider: 6{,}716 tracks, 25 XMLs -- all
matched) and that both acquisition CLIs (\texttt{soaring-para}/\texttt{soaring-delta
status}) still work against the new layout, since every acquisition module already went
through \texttt{Config}'s path properties with no hard-coded paths elsewhere. The
previously-local \texttt{track\_scan.parquet} copies moved to
\texttt{<data\_root>/derived/} (read back and row/column-checked after the move); the
orphaned local \texttt{catalog.csv} copies (superseded by the track-based scan months
ago, confirmed unreferenced by any code) were removed.

\emph{Figure fixes, from a closer look at the newly-cached data.} Figure~4.5 (gaps): the
adopted \texttt{max\_gap\_factor} was raised from 5 to 10 -- at 5 it retained only
77.8\,\%/65.0\,\% of para/hang flights (nowhere near the near-full retention of the
duration/path cuts), at 10 it reaches 86.9\,\%/85.1\,\%. Considered 20 (90.8\,\%/90.6\,\%,
little further gain) and rejected it: the cut is \emph{relative} to each flight's own
native $\Delta t$, so for the slower hang-glider loggers (up to 10 s native) a
factor of 20 would tolerate interpolating across a $\sim$200\,s gap -- long enough to
span a thermal climb, not a sane linear-interpolation span. Also tightened the
distribution panels' axis range (most mass sits under the 90th percentile; the old
0--200/0--0.6 range was mostly empty). Figure~4.1 (altitude noise): panel (a)'s
difference trace now uses a colour distinct from both channels (was reusing GNSS's red);
its window grew from 10 to 30 minutes; and a Nyquist-frequency line was added to panel
(b) (previously discussed in the caption but never drawn). Along the way, found and fixed
a real bug in the ``representative flight'' selection: it picked the first flight
matching the PSD criteria, later changed to the \emph{longest} by elapsed time -- until a
30-hour, 1{,}100-fix outlier (one corrupted timestamp gap) exposed that elapsed time
is not robust to a single bad gap. Fixed to select by \emph{fix count} instead, which
does not have this failure mode.

\paragraph{2026-07 --- Fix-level cleaning made data-driven; parser roll-over bug; figure and data-folder cleanups.}
\emph{Fix-level cleaning (Table~4.1) is now audited on the data, like the flight-level cuts.}
Added a figure to Sec.~4.1.2 (fix-level cleaning): the per-\emph{fix} distributions of
horizontal speed, barometric vertical speed and barometric altitude
(\texttt{make\_fixlevel\_diagnostics\_figure}, from \texttt{fix\_level\_distributions} over a
seeded per-fix sample -- millions of fixes). The point that is easy to get wrong: a fix-level
bound removes single fixes from an otherwise good flight, so what justifies it is the fraction
of \emph{fixes} it removes, not the number of flights it touches (a bound may touch many flights
harmlessly). Each bound sits in the sparse, implausible tail. The per-flight \emph{maxima}
already in the scan (\texttt{max\_vxy\_mps} and friends) answer the wrong question -- flights
touched -- so they do not place the cuts.

\emph{Removed the absolute 60 s inter-fix time-gap from the fix-level table.} It duplicated the
sampling-regularity cut of Sec.~4.1.6, which is more principled: relative to each flight's own
native $\Delta t$, so it scales with the logger's rate. Gaps are now handled in exactly one
place. Neither was ever an enforced filter (both are declared thresholds for the still-to-build
cleaning routine), so this is a spec simplification, not a behaviour change.

\emph{Found and fixed the root cause of the ``30-hour, 1{,}100-fix'' pathology} worked around in
the previous entry. The real bug was in the IGC parser's midnight roll-over unwrap, which
treated \emph{any} backward step in the time-of-day as a new day and added 86{,}400 s: a
few-second GPS glitch became a spurious multi-hour gap and a wildly wrong duration. Now only a
drop of (nearly) a whole day counts as a roll-over, and residual backward jitter is clamped so
the series stays monotonic. Added regression tests for both the true roll-over and the glitch.

\emph{Figure and config cleanups.} The altitude-noise figure (Sec.~4.1.1) is now four panels:
(a) the two raw channels overlaid on one window -- they track the same climb, GNSS visibly
noisier -- beside (b) their difference, then (c) the PSD and (d) the fallback rate; this also
fixed the old a/b/d panel labelling. The gap-diagnostics figure (Sec.~4.1.6): the distribution
panels no longer clip the tail into an artificial spike at the edge, and their x-range is fitted
to the data's own percentiles instead of a fixed constant. On principle -- the code runs on
datasets of different sources and rates -- result-specific numbers (retention percentages,
one-off anecdotes) were pruned from the YAML and code comments; such numbers belong in the
thesis, not in reusable config.

\emph{Local data folder clarified.} The two versioned \texttt{seasons\_index.csv} are the
offline reproducibility anchor for the thesis's dataset counts -- they let the document build
with no SSD mounted -- not stray local data. Their sync from the canonical SSD copy is now
explicit and automatic (\texttt{refresh\_seasons\_index.py}, run best-effort by the pre-commit
hook), so the snapshot cannot silently drift; running it immediately caught a stale paraglider
snapshot (only the line endings differed -- the counts were identical). Rewrote
\texttt{data/README.md} and fixed several stale references to a flat
\texttt{data/seasons\_index.csv} path that never existed.

\paragraph{2026-07 --- Coordinate/geodesy figures.}
Redrew the coordinate-transformation figures: Figure~4.3 (WGS84 ellipsoid; geodetic vs
geocentric latitude) and Figure~4.4 (the ECEF$\to$ENU rotation, in our notation), and added the
ESA Navipedia citation for the ECEF conversion.

\paragraph{2026-06 --- Documentation.}
Project docs are built with MkDocs and published on GitHub Pages. Initially deployed with
\texttt{mkdocs gh-deploy}, which accumulated one built-site commit per deploy on a
\texttt{gh-pages} branch; migrated to the official GitHub Pages \emph{artifact} workflow
(build $\to$ upload artifact $\to$ deploy), removing the branch and keeping the git history
clean.

\paragraph{Operational note.} In this repo \texttt{uv sync} occasionally fails to
(re)install the editable package; \texttt{uv sync --reinstall-package soaring} fixes it.

\section{Next steps}
These mirror the planning chapter of the state document
(\texttt{thesis/sections/04-next-steps}) and are kept in sync with it. The near-term plan
is to analyse the data \emph{in parallel} with studying the segmentation, since many
observables can be built before any phase splitting.

\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Data sources.} FFVL paraglider and hang-glider CFD data are collected,
        cataloged and verified. Extend to further sources -- notably WeGlide for sailplanes
        (a higher-quota request is pending) and possibly XContest -- merging into one catalog
        and stratifying by source. Refresh the per-discipline \texttt{seasons\_index.csv} via
        \texttt{build-catalog} so the document statistics stay current.
  \item \textbf{Pre-processing.} The parser is in place (\texttt{soaring.analysis.igc}) and
        the pipeline is designed and thresholded (\texttt{configs/preprocessing.yaml}):
        fix-level cleaning (great-circle speeds), $v_{xy}$-based trimming, data-driven
        flight-level filtering (duration $+$ path length), ENU conversion (barometric
        vertical), native-$\Delta t$ uniformity, and Savitzky--Golay
        smoothing/differentiation. Next: implement it end-to-end (parser $\to\dots\to$ Parquet
        \texttt{fixes} table) per the blueprint, and measure the ENU power spectra to fix the
        Savitzky--Golay window.
  \item \textbf{Whole-trajectory observables (no segmentation).} Immediate next step:
        characterise the data with observables that need no phases --- MSD and its scaling
        exponent, the displacement distribution and its tails, the velocity autocorrelation
        and turning-angle distribution, the speed/vertical-velocity distributions, and
        geometric measures (tortuosity, fractal dimension, radius of gyration). Goal: a
        first physical/statistical picture and some preliminary findings.
  \item \textbf{Segmentation.} Study J\'er\'emie's internship report and code, then
        re-examine the transition/search/climb HMM (binary features: sign of vertical
        speed, curvature-angle persistence, straightness) --- decide whether to keep that
        approach or add complexity for a better segmentation on our larger, more
        heterogeneous data.
  \item \textbf{Phase-resolved observables.} After segmentation, extract step lengths,
        waiting times, search durations, per-phase kinematics and the angular diffusion
        between successive glides.
  \item \textbf{Reproduce the reference.} Check that the enlarged dataset reproduces
        Vilpellet et al.: per-phase conditional MSD (their Fig.~3) and per-phase
        descriptive statistics (their Fig.~4), plus the global $H\approx0.88$ ---
        validation + universality test.
  \item \textbf{Transport analysis.} Estimate the MSD scaling exponent, fit the tails of
        the waiting-time and step-length distributions, and perform CTRW-vs-L\'evy-walk
        model selection.
  \item \textbf{Minimal model.} A minimal stochastic model for $H\approx0.88$: resolve
        transition segments, merge search+climb into one effective state, with angular
        diffusion between successive transition directions (preliminary idea, to be
        refined/changed).
\end{itemize}

\end{document}
